name: Piornal Pass from Valdastillas
name_original: Piornal desde Valdastillas — ida y vuelta
description: Cycle the Piornal Pass from Valdastillas, a moderate 21.0-mile road cycling route with 2723 feet of elevation gain and scenic valley views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valdastillas traverse the Jerte Valley, characterized by its mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. The region features numerous passes and climbs, offering challenging ascents and descents for road cyclists. River valleys and forested areas provide varied scenery throughout the routes. This landscape ensures a diverse experience, from demanding climbs to more moderate valley rides. Best road cycling routes around Valdastillas The most popular road cycling route is Piornal Pass from Valdastillas, a 21.0 miles (33.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a substantial climb to the Piornal Pass. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Honduras Pass – Puerto de Béjar loop from Asador Hotel Balneario Valle del Jerte, a difficult 95.3 miles (153.3 km) path. This long-distance route includes multiple mountain passes and extensive climbs through the mountainous landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Plasencia Aqueduct – San Estebán Gate loop from Valdastillas, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Valdastillas is defined by challenging mountain passes, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to advanced long-distance routes. The routes in Valdastillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdastillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valdastillas?
A: Valdastillas offers over 30 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse selection, from moderate climbs to challenging long-distance tours through the Jerte Valley.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Valdastillas?
A: While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. Most routes, however, are moderate to difficult, featuring significant elevation changes. Beginners should look for routes primarily within the valley floor for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of challenging climbs can advanced road cyclists expect in Valdastillas?
A: Advanced cyclists will find numerous challenging climbs and mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass – Puerto de Béjar loop&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive ascents and descents over long distances, testing endurance and climbing skills. The region's topography ensures a demanding ride for experienced riders.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Valdastillas?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Valdastillas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Aqueduct – San Estebán Gate loop from Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that combines varied terrain with historical landmarks.

Q: What are the best times of year for road cycling in Valdastillas?
A: The Jerte Valley is particularly beautiful in spring when the cherry blossoms are in bloom, offering stunning scenery for cyclists. Autumn also provides pleasant temperatures and vibrant fall colors. Summers can be hot, especially in the valley, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during this season. Winters are generally mild but can bring colder temperatures to higher passes.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by or offer access to stunning natural attractions. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley&lt;/a&gt;. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2842817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2684422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offer spectacular vistas.

Q: Are there any routes that include historical landmarks or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Aqueduct – San Estebán Gate loop from Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through varied terrain and past significant historical sites, offering a blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Valdastillas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the diverse scenery of river valleys and rolling hills, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in Valdastillas?
A: While popular routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292300456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Pass from Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt; are well-loved, exploring some of the less-trafficked routes can lead to unique discoveries. Consider routes that venture deeper into the forested areas or along smaller river tributaries for a more secluded experience. The region's extensive network means there's always something new to uncover.

Q: What are the typical road conditions like for cycling in Valdastillas?
A: The road conditions in Valdastillas vary. Main roads are generally well-maintained, but smaller, more rural roads, especially those leading to higher passes or through less populated areas, might have older asphalt or occasional rough patches. Always be prepared for varied surfaces, particularly on descents.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coffee stops or places to refuel along the way?
A: Many villages and towns within the Jerte Valley, including Valdastillas itself, offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel. When planning longer routes, it's advisable to check the map for towns or hamlets along your chosen path to ensure you have opportunities for coffee breaks and supplies.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Valdastillas is a great starting point for long-distance road cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass – Puerto de Béjar loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, covering over 95 miles (153 km) with significant elevation, perfect for those seeking an epic day in the saddle.
tips: A very pleasant climb of 9.2 km from Valdastillas to Piornal gaining 525 m with a comfortable gradient of 5.7%. Numerous curves to enjoy towards the summit at 1,150 meters above sea level.
A charming mountain town located at 1,150 meters above sea level that has an incredible view over the Jerte valley.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Piornal Pass from Valdastillas cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, but it involves a significant and sustained climb. The ascent to Puerto del Piornal covers 13.3 km with a positive slope of 692 meters and an average gradient of 5.2%. It requires good to very good fitness and is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly road, with sections of quiet road and asphalt. However, a significant portion is described as 'undefined' surface, and some parts are 'track,' which might imply less-than-perfect road conditions. Be prepared for varied road surfaces, typical for mountain passes in the region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 33.8 km long with an estimated cycling time of about 2 hours and 3 minutes (7390 seconds). This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available in Valdastillas?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Valdastillas. While specific parking details aren't provided, Valdastillas is a village, and you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village to begin your ride. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

faqs.question: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the challenging Climb from Valdastillas to Piornal. Once you reach Piornal, you'll be rewarded with a stunning View of the Jerte Valley from Piornal. The broader Extremadura region offers natural beauty and passes through various villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Piornal Pass from Valdastillas?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Always check the weather forecast before you go, especially for mountain passes.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Piornal Pass from Valdastillas. It's a public road cycling route.

faqs.question: Is the Piornal Pass from Valdastillas route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany you on the bike itself. If you plan to bring a dog, they would need to be secured in a bike trailer designed for pets, but this route's challenging climbs might make that difficult. It's not a typical 'dog-friendly' trail in the hiking sense.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through villages like Valdastillas and Piornal, you can expect to find opportunities for refreshments, such as cafes or small shops. Piornal, being a larger village, will likely offer more options for food and possibly accommodation.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the [CIMA CC02] Piornal * Valdastillas route (68%) and also includes sections of the [CIMA CC02] Piornal * Cabrero (32%), Cordel del Valle, and Ruta del Mojón Blanco, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any local tips for tackling the Piornal Pass climb?
faqs.answer: The climb to Piornal Pass is a significant challenge. Pace yourself, especially on the sustained 5.2% average gradient over 13.3 km. Ensure your bike is in good condition, and consider starting early in the day to avoid the midday sun, particularly in warmer months. Bring sufficient water and nutrition.

waypoints.name: View of the Jerte Valley from Piornal
